exam_format: Computer-based testing (CBT), fixed-form. 100-150 questions depending on exam. Scaled scoring (390 passing out of 500).
exam_price_notes: Eligibility requirements include a high school diploma or GED, and completion of a training program or work experience in the field.
exam_registration_url: Create an Account to Get Started

testing_centers.provider: PSI or NHA-approved test centers / online proctoring
testing_centers.notes: Most exams are computer-based (linear fixed-form). Some available via live remote proctoring. NCCA accredited. Results often immediate or within days.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required at check-in. Must match name on registration exactly.
exam_rules: Closed book. No notes, phones, or reference materials. Calculators provided on-screen if needed. Follow all test center or remote proctoring rules.
renewal_options: Every 2 years via online portal. Requires 10 continuing education (CE) credits per cycle. CE can be earned through NHA courses or approved providers. Renewal fee applies.
target_audience: Healthcare professionals and individuals seeking to become electronic health records specialists.
recommended_experience: Completion of an electronic health records specialist training program within the last 5 years, or 1-2 years of supervised work experience in the field.

prerequisites.description: High school diploma or GED, or scheduled to earn one within the next 12 months.
Completion of an electronic health records specialist training program within the last 5 years, or 1-2 years of supervised work experience in the field.

retake_policy.notes: Full exam fee charged for each retake. No limit specified beyond waiting period.

objectives.title: Auditing patient records for compliance
sub_objectives: Ensure accuracy and completeness of patient data

objectives.title: Abstracting clinical information for reports
sub_objectives: Extract relevant clinical data from patient records
Prepare reports for internal or external use

objectives.title: Performing basic coding for reimbursement claims
Processing Release of Information (ROI) requests
sub_objectives: Ensure proper authorization for release of information

objectives.title: Reviewing patient demographic and insurance information
sub_objectives: Ensure insurance information is up to date

objectives.title: Discussing patient information with physicians and insurance professionals
sub_objectives: Ensure accurate and appropriate sharing of information

official_study_resources.notes: Provides contemporary learning tools such as practice drills, audio and video content, and review questions.

official_study_resources.title: CEHRS Practice Tests
official_study_resources.notes: Get up to five attempts on a CEHRS practice test.

official_study_resources.title: CEHRS Preparation Package
official_study_resources.notes: Includes robust interactive study materials and a CEHRS study guide, along with the CEHRS practice test.

official_study_resources.title: CEHRS Test Plan
official_study_resources.notes: Free study aide that maps out key tasks and knowledge needed for the CEHRS exam.

aliases: certified electronic health records specialist cehrs
